complaints: I waste 15 minutes every afternoon scrolling Swiggy before defaulting to the same three places.
By 4pm I have no energy left to decide what to eat, so I just impulse-order whatever is fastest.
I already order in every day — I just don't want to keep choosing.
My meal prep is gone by Wednesday and I fall back onto off-plan delivery.
No delivery app tells me the protein or calories of a specific restaurant dish.
I track my macros religiously but still fail because I can't order food that fits them.
pain_points: No macro visibility on restaurant menus
Gap between tracking and procuring food

complaints: My monthly Swiggy bill is ₹10-15k and I genuinely can't account for it.
Prices spike exactly when I'm hungriest, so I pay the most for the worst choices.
I want to eat out daily but not blow past a budget I set for myself.
pain_points: No spend cap on recurring ordering

recommendations: Lead with health/adherence value (proven ₹1,000-1,700/mo WTP), use convenience as the acquisition hook, keep budget as a guardrail feature.
Ship one-tap-approve per order, not silent autonomy — money plus allergens make full auto-ordering too risky at launch.
Never charge a convenience surcharge; monetize a health-framed subscription plus affiliate economics.
Reserve the cross-platform orchestration seam (Swiggy + Zomato + Instamart + tiffin) as the defensible moat; v1 is Swiggy-only behind a swappable adapter.
Deliver macros via a hybrid pipeline (LLM canonicalize plus an India-native food database) and frame every figure as an estimate.
